title: monitoring — helper functions
description: Ergonomic helpers for firing alerts, resolving providers, and reading dashboard state. All helpers are thin wrappers around bound services — never business logic. Callers who need the wire-shape of alerts use the FireAlertJob path; helpers here are for imperative flows (support tools, tests, ad-hoc scripts).

helpers.purpose: Imperative fire path. Returns the created MonitoringAlert OR null when the policy's suppression_config matched. Wraps FireAlertJob::dispatchSync() for imperative flows. Reserved for support tooling — evaluator-driven flow uses FireAlertJob via the queue.

Returns the driver instance for a provider_config row. Delegates to MonitoringProviderManager::forConfig() which reads $config->provider + $config->config and calls the appropriate createXxxDriver factory. Reserved for advanced consumers — typical dispatch flows go through NotifyProvidersJob.

Returns the active provider_configs for (tenant, severity) — is_active=true AND severity IN enabled_severities AND circuit_breaker_open_until IS NULL OR <= now(). Used by MonitoringPagingCoordinator AND by support tooling that pre-computes the fan-out plan.

Manually execute a health check + return the resulting HealthCheckRun row. Delegates to HealthCheckExecutor::execute($check). Used by the POST /health-checks/{h}/run endpoint + tests. Rate-limited by monitoring.rate_limit middleware.

Builds the { signature, timestamp } pair used by CustomWebhookProvider. Signature = SHA256(secret + timestamp + payload). timestamp defaults to time() when omitted. Consumers add these to request headers.

Returns the aggregated dashboard read-model for a tenant: { health_checks_summary, active_alerts_by_severity, open_incidents_count, mttr_last_7d_seconds, top_failing_checks, provider_health }. Read from the mon:dashboard:{tenant_id} cache OR computed on-the-fly + cached 60s. Feeds GET /dashboard endpoint.
Mean-Time-To-Resolve in seconds over the trailing $days days for a tenant. Feeds SLO reporting + the dashboard read-model + weekly digest.
Mean-Time-To-Acknowledge in seconds over the trailing $days days.

notes: Helpers deliberately delegate — testing swaps the bound service to a stub, and every helper follows.
No helper directly touches the DB — everything routes through repositories or services. Keeps the boundary clean.
The fire_monitoring_alert helper is intentionally NOT the primary fire path. Production code uses the evaluator → FireAlertJob path; the helper exists for support scripts + tests + one-off backfills.
